sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So the middle row is back to its original state: w, b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g. The green cross shifted it right, undoing the left shift. Good.
Now let me understand the full layout:
- Top row (5 squares): S, b, p, Y, p
- Middle row (10 squares): w, b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g
- Bottom row (10 squares): w, S, b, w, b, S, p, b, g, S
- Left column (7 squares): p, g, w, w, Y, g, S
- Right column (6 squares): b, S, w, g, w, b
- Bottom row (5 squares): S, S, p, p, g
Wait, I need to re-examine. The left and right "columns" are actually between the horizontal rows. Let me think about this as a grid of squares:
Row positions (2x2 squares):
- Top: row 17, cols 23, 26, 29, 32, 35 (5 squares)
- Middle: row 26, cols 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44 (10 squares)
- Bottom: row 35, cols 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44 (10 squares)
Between top and middle:
- Left: row 20, col 23; row 23, col 23
- Right: row 20, col 35; row 23, col 35
Between middle and bottom:
- Left: row 29, col 23; row 32, col 23
- Right: row 29, col 35; row 32, col 35
Below bottom:
- Left: row 38, col 23; row 41, col 23
- Right: row 38, col 35; row 41, col 35
- Bottom row: row 44, cols 23, 26, 29, 32, 35 (5 squares)
So the structure is like a cross with 3 horizontal rows and 2 vertical columns (left and right), plus a bottom row.
The "38 target actions" hint is interesting. Let me think about what the goal could be. Maybe the goal is to make all columns match some pattern, or to align the rows so that certain columns have matching colors.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The level 1 had 20 perimeter squares and the goal was to shift them until the yellow-framed positions had matching colors. In level 2, maybe the goal is similar but with multiple independent rows.
Let me look at the yellow markers more carefully. The 1x1 Y markers form a frame at cols 34-37, rows 25-37. This frame surrounds the center area where the middle and bottom rows overlap.
Actually, I think the yellow 1x1 markers might indicate which squares are "active" or "target" positions. Let me look at which squares are inside this frame:
- The frame is at cols 34-37, rows 25-37
- This covers the right portion of the middle and bottom rows
Hmm, but the yellow 2x2 squares at (17,32) and (38,23) are actual colored squares, not frames. Let me reconsider what the goal is.
Let me try a different approach. Let me click the top-left red cross to see what it does.
